detection_signals: Oxidative aging is always worse than protected aging
Wines aged under flor are always superior in quality
Oloroso is a lower quality version of Fino because it's oxidized
why_incorrect: Oxidative and biological ageing produce different flavor profiles. However, the sensory results are not universally better or worse—they are stylistically different.
misconception: Oxidative ageing and biological ageing (flor protection) always produce wines with distinctly different flavors, and one is always better than the other.
corrected_understanding: Oxidative and biological ageing produce distinct flavor profiles. Neither is inherently superior; they are stylistic choices producing different quality expressions.
